faq.title: Solar Energy FAQ for Panama
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before installing your solar panels.

items.question: How much does solar installation cost in Panama?
items.answer: A residential system (3-15 kW) typically costs between $4,000 and $20,000 depending on roof size, consumption, and equipment type. Thanks to Law 417, solar equipment is imported tax-free, lowering the cost. Request a free quote for your exact price.

items.question: How much can I save on my electricity bill?
items.answer: Savings depend on your daytime usage and utility tariff. Our clients in Azuero went from paying $280 to $35 per month — up to 95% savings. We calculate your real savings from your bill, not a generic percentage.

items.question: What is Law 417 and how does it benefit me?
items.answer: Law 417 is Panama's renewable energy incentive that allows importing solar equipment with no import duties or ITBMS tax. This significantly reduces the total cost of your system. We handle the entire process for you.

items.question: What is the payback period for solar?
items.answer: With Panama's electricity tariffs, the typical payback is 3 to 5 years for residential and commercial systems. After that, you enjoy nearly free electricity for over 20 years, since panels carry a 25-year warranty.

items.question: Do solar panels work during the rainy season?
items.answer: Yes. Modern panels generate electricity even on cloudy days, producing 10% to 25% of peak capacity. Panama receives abundant sunshine year-round, especially in the Azuero Peninsula.

items.question: How long does installation take?
items.answer: Most residential systems are installed in a single day. Commercial systems (30 kW+) take 3 to 7 days. Our certified team delivers a clean, professional installation with remote monitoring included.

items.question: What warranty do you offer?
items.answer: We install Tier-1 panels with a 25-year performance warranty and inverters with 10 to 25 years of protection. Each proposal lists equipment warranties and Solaris workmanship coverage in writing.

items.question: Do I need permits to install solar panels?
items.answer: Yes, grid-connected systems require utility approval and, in many cases, ASEP approval. Solaris handles the entire interconnection process for you — from application to final connection.
